title: Meltingly Gooey Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A salty and gooey Moroccan dish featuring pomegranate molasses, perfect for a sick day, and kosher friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the pomegranate molasses, olive oil, cumin, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the chopped onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger to the bowl and stir to combine.
Add the chicken thighs to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Transfer the chicken and marinade to a large Dutch oven or heavy pot with a tight-fitting lid.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 1/2 hours, or until the chicken is tender and the sauce is thick and gooey.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Serve the chicken and sauce hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
